text:
Redwood (レドウッド, Redouddo) was a Greed Island player Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 132 and a Hunter. 

Appearance

Redwood was a man of average height, with fair hair neatly combed back, a thick beard, and a button nose.

Plot

Greed Island arc

Redwood is among a group of new players that were convinced to join Nickes' Alliance, presumably by Contarch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 Along with the other new recruits, Redwood waits in front of the Shiso Tree for Jispa's arrival. The moment Jispa arrives, he instantly uses an "Accompany" to transport them to Kosofftro, who sits somewhere near the alliance base. As the new recruits are led to the base by Jispa, he divulges to them that their first task is to collect certain spell cards in Masadora for the alliance. At the base, the new recruits are instructed to memorize a list of spell cards within an hour. 

When the alliance is close to collecting all of the cards in the game, Genthru reveals himself to be the Bomber and claims that he has placed bombs on all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 After Genthru escapes the alliance base using a "Leave" card, all the members begin to panic from the revelation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 Left with no other options after Jispa's death, the alliance members surrender all of their spell cards to the Bombers, hoping that they will disarm the bombs attached to them. However, in an act of deception, the Bombers simultaneously set off the bombs and killed nearly all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 

Redwood's name is listed in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Redwood is capable enough to have passed the notoriously difficult Hunter Exam. However, since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Redwood also had none. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Redwood was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Redwood's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook.
